chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The Kindness with the prisoner and feeding him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/365)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/365)
text: الْأَسِيرُ طَعَامُهُ عَلَى مَنْ أَسَرَهُ حَقٌّ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ كَافِراً يُقْتَلُ مِنَ الْغَدِ فَإِنَّهُ يَنْبَغِي لَهُ أَنْ يَرْؤُفَهُ وَ يُطْعِمَهُ وَ يَسْقِيَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Kufy, from Hamdan Al qalanasy, from Muhammad Bin Al Waleed, from Aban Bin Usman, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The prisoner, his meals are upon the one who imprisons him, a right upon him, and even he was an Infidel to be killed the next day, for it is befitting for him that he is kind to him, and feeds him, and quenches him’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Kufiy has narrated from Hamdan al-Qalanisiy from Muhammad ibn al-Walid from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Mansur ibn Hazim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that feeding the prisoner is upon the one who has captured him, even if he is an unbeliever whom he wants to eliminate the next day. He should be kind to him, feed him and quench his thirst.”
